description_long: This is a military-style rifle with a robust metal receiver and a wooden fixed stock, showing signs of wear and use. The rifle features a long, cylindrical barrel with a front sight post and a detachable curved box magazine, typical of mid-20th-century assault rifles. The handguard and pistol grip are also made of wood, contrasting with the dark metallic finish of the main body. The magazine is slightly curved, indicating it is designed for intermediate cartridges. The surface of the metal components appears matte with some areas of gloss from wear, while the wood has a dark, weathered appearance. The overall design is utilitarian, built for durability and reliability in urban or combat environments.
description: A worn, military-style rifle with a metal body, wooden stock and grip, and a curved detachable magazine. The finish is matte with visible wear on both metal and wood parts.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the rifle, including the wooden stock, metal receiver, curved magazine, and barrel with front sight.
description_view_1: Front-on view highlighting the barrel's end, front sight post, and the narrow profile of the rifle.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the wooden stock, metal receiver, curved magazine, and barrel, mirroring view 0 but from the other side.
description_view_3: Rear view focusing on the butt of the wooden stock and the alignment of the barrel, showing the rifle's slim profile.
